sources.reviewedCode:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(1)

# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: At each position, ask how many legal ways the remainder can be interpreted if you consume one or two digits.
class Solution(object):
    def numDecodings(self, s):
        """
        :type s: str
        :rtype: int
        """
        if len(s) == 0 or s[0] == '0':
            return 0
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Position DP, branching on one-step versus two-step moves, and handling invalid local patterns cleanly.
        prev, prev_prev = 1, 0
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for i in xrange(len(s)):
            cur = 0
            # [Reader note] Decision point
            # Pay attention here: The challenge is less about coding and more about being precise about which local chunks are legal.
            if s[i] != '0':
                cur = prev
            if i > 0 and (s[i - 1] == '1' or (s[i - 1] == '2' and s[i] <= '6')):
                cur += prev_prev
            prev, prev_prev = cur, prev
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return prev

sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: At each position, ask how many legal ways the remainder can be interpreted if you consume one or two digits.
class Solution:
    def numDecodings(self, s: str) -> int:
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Position DP, branching on one-step versus two-step moves, and handling invalid local patterns cleanly.
        n = len(s)
        f = [1] + [0] * n
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for i, c in enumerate(s, 1):
            # [Reader note] Decision point
            # Pay attention here: The challenge is less about coding and more about being precise about which local chunks are legal.
            if c != "0":
                f[i] = f[i - 1]
            if i > 1 and s[i - 2] != "0" and int(s[i - 2 : i]) <= 26:
                f[i] += f[i - 2]
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return f[n]

writeups.title: 091.Decode-Ways
writeups.excerpt: ### 091.Decode-Ways 非常典型的DP问题。dp[i]= dp[i-2] （如果最后两位合法） + dp[i-1] （如果最后一位合法） 最后两位合法存在，指的是数字在10和26之间；最后一位合法存在，指的是不能为0. 为了方便书写，可以将字符串额外在首端添加一个零。这样dp[i]表示的就是s[i]的第i位所代表的结果，且dp[0]是边界条件。 另外特别注意的是： 1. 边界条件dp[0]=1而不是0. 2. dp[1]需要单独计算。dp[2]以后可以有通用表达式。 [Leetcode Link](https://leetcode.com/problems/decode-ways)
